data.content_ref: inline:The target identifies a missing school-to-route binding but fails to address the critical vulnerability of 'School Identity Spoofing' in the adapter layer. Even if the scheduler were hardened with a hardcoded mapping (as suggested), the current adapter implementation (src/deepreason/llm/adapter.py) accepts a 'school' parameter from the calling context without cryptographic verification against the active lease. A compromised or prompt-injected upstream component could invoke the adapter with a valid route lease but a falsified school identity, causing the model to receive incorrect conditioning context. This violates C1 (code control) because the binding relies on trust in the caller rather than an immutable, verified manifest assertion at the point of execution. The proposed fix of only updating the scheduler is insufficient; the adapter must enforce that the school identity in the request matches the school bound to the route in the immutable manifest, rejecting any mismatch regardless of the scheduler's intent.
